articles.title: Heavy showers continue in coastal districts of Maharashtra - News On AIR
articles.full_text:
Heavy showers continue in the coastal districts of Maharashtra, with Mumbai recording a total of 588 mm of rainfall over the past four days. This accounts for 74% of the total rainfall expected in the month of July. Vikhroli, in the Mumbai suburbs, recorded the highest rainfall of 316 mm in the last 24 hours. High tide conditions coinciding with continuous rainfall will keep authorities on high alert in the Mumbai region and Thane. BMC has said around 10,000 staff are active on the ground to ensure civic life remains smooth.
The India Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for Mumbai, Thane, Palghar and the ghat areas of Pune and Satara for the next two days, while an Orange Alert remains in force for the ghat areas of Raigad, Ratnagiri and Nashik. NDRF remains deployed in sensitive districts to ensure swift action. In Raigad, major rivers, including the Savitri, Kundalika and Ulhas, are flowing at full capacity, while waterlogging was reported in Mahad.
Heavy rain in the Konkan region has significantly improved water storage in major dams across Thane, Palghar, Raigad, Ratnagiri and Sindhudurg. Continuous rain has also raised water levels in the Panchganga and other rivers in Kolhapur. Jalna received widespread rainfall, providing relief to Kharif crops, while Akola witnessed light showers.
